title: Calliandra cruziana
taxonomy: Calliandra cruziana is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. This species is part of the genus Calliandra, which comprises around 200 species of plants. The genus Calliandra is a group of legumes native to the Americas, often referred to as 'powderpuff trees' or 'powder puff trees' due to their fluffy, pink or red flowers. The classification of Calliandra cruziana is further specified as belonging to the subfamily Faboideae. Its classification reflects its evolutionary relationships and position within the plant kingdom.

intro: Calliandra cruziana, also known as "Estramadura" or "Mexican Powder Puff," is a species of flowering plant in the pea family (Fabaceae), characterized by its showy, powder-puff-like flowers and medicinal properties. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. Bioactive constituents of Calliandra cruziana include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. The plant is commonly used to make herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and capsules, which are used to relieve respiratory symptoms and promote overall well-being.
